# dokploy_project (Data Source)

Fetches an existing Dokploy project by its id or name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up a project by id
data "dokploy_project" "by_id" {
  id = "abc123"
}

# Look up a project by name
data "dokploy_project" "by_name" {
  name = "My Project"
}

output "project_environments" {
  value = data.dokploy_project.by_name.environments
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the project. Either id or name must be set.
- `name` (String) The display name of the project. Either name or id must be set.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) The description of the project.
- `environments` (Attributes List) The environments belonging to the project. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments))

<a id="nestedatt--environments"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) The description of the environment.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the environment.
- `name` (String) The name of the environment.
